=== Metadata ===
Effect version: 3.19.19

/.src/preferSchemaOverJson.ts(5,45): suggestion TS377026: Consider using Effect Schema for JSON operations instead of JSON.parse/JSON.stringify. effect(preferSchemaOverJson)
/.src/preferSchemaOverJson.ts(8,31): warning TS377022: The 'catch' callback in Effect.try returns global 'Error', which loses type safety as untagged errors merge together. Consider using a tagged error and optionally wrapping the original in a 'cause' property. effect(globalErrorInEffectCatch)
/.src/preferSchemaOverJson.ts(9,14): suggestion TS377026: Consider using Effect Schema for JSON operations instead of JSON.parse/JSON.stringify. effect(preferSchemaOverJson)
/.src/preferSchemaOverJson.ts(10,16): warning TS377023: Global 'Error' loses type safety as untagged errors merge together in the Effect failure channel. Consider using a tagged error and optionally wrapping the original in a 'cause' property. effect(globalErrorInEffectFailure)
/.src/preferSchemaOverJson.ts(14,49): suggestion TS377026: Consider using Effect Schema for JSON operations instead of JSON.parse/JSON.stringify. effect(preferSchemaOverJson)
/.src/preferSchemaOverJson.ts(17,35): warning TS377022: The 'catch' callback in Effect.try returns global 'Error', which loses type safety as untagged errors merge together. Consider using a tagged error and optionally wrapping the original in a 'cause' property. effect(globalErrorInEffectCatch)
/.src/preferSchemaOverJson.ts(18,14): suggestion TS377026: Consider using Effect Schema for JSON operations instead of JSON.parse/JSON.stringify. effect(preferSchemaOverJson)
/.src/preferSchemaOverJson.ts(19,16): warning TS377023: Global 'Error' loses type safety as untagged errors merge together in the Effect failure channel. Consider using a tagged error and optionally wrapping the original in a 'cause' property. effect(globalErrorInEffectFailure)
/.src/preferSchemaOverJson.ts(25,18): suggestion TS377026: Consider using Effect Schema for JSON operations instead of JSON.parse/JSON.stringify. effect(preferSchemaOverJson)
/.src/preferSchemaOverJson.ts(33,16): suggestion TS377026: Consider using Effect Schema for JSON operations instead of JSON.parse/JSON.stringify. effect(preferSchemaOverJson)
/.src/preferSchemaOverJson.ts(41,18): suggestion TS377026: Consider using Effect Schema for JSON operations instead of JSON.parse/JSON.stringify. effect(preferSchemaOverJson)


==== /.src/preferSchemaOverJson.ts (11 errors) ====
    // @effect-v3
    import { Effect } from "effect"
    
    // Should trigger diagnostic - JSON.parse inside Effect.try (simple form)
    export const parseSimple = Effect.try(() => JSON.parse("{\"name\":\"John\"}"))
                                                ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! suggestion TS377026: Consider using Effect Schema for JSON operations instead of JSON.parse/JSON.stringify. effect(preferSchemaOverJson)
    
    // Should trigger diagnostic - JSON.parse inside Effect.try (object form)
    export const parseWithCatch = Effect.try({
                                  ~~~~~~~~~~
!!! warning TS377022: The 'catch' callback in Effect.try returns global 'Error', which loses type safety as untagged errors merge together. Consider using a tagged error and optionally wrapping the original in a 'cause' property. effect(globalErrorInEffectCatch)
      try: () => JSON.parse("{\"name\":\"Jane\"}"),
                 ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! suggestion TS377026: Consider using Effect Schema for JSON operations instead of JSON.parse/JSON.stringify. effect(preferSchemaOverJson)
      catch: () => new Error("Failed to parse")
                   ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! warning TS377023: Global 'Error' loses type safety as untagged errors merge together in the Effect failure channel. Consider using a tagged error and optionally wrapping the original in a 'cause' property. effect(globalErrorInEffectFailure)
    })
    
    // Should trigger diagnostic - JSON.stringify inside Effect.try (simple form)
    export const stringifySimple = Effect.try(() => JSON.stringify({ name: "Bob" }))
                                                    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! suggestion TS377026: Consider using Effect Schema for JSON operations instead of JSON.parse/JSON.stringify. effect(preferSchemaOverJson)
    
    // Should trigger diagnostic - JSON.stringify inside Effect.try (object form)
    export const stringifyWithCatch = Effect.try({
                                      ~~~~~~~~~~
!!! warning TS377022: The 'catch' callback in Effect.try returns global 'Error', which loses type safety as untagged errors merge together. Consider using a tagged error and optionally wrapping the original in a 'cause' property. effect(globalErrorInEffectCatch)
      try: () => JSON.stringify({ name: "Alice" }),
                 ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! suggestion TS377026: Consider using Effect Schema for JSON operations instead of JSON.parse/JSON.stringify. effect(preferSchemaOverJson)
      catch: () => new Error("Failed to stringify")
                   ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! warning TS377023: Global 'Error' loses type safety as untagged errors merge together in the Effect failure channel. Consider using a tagged error and optionally wrapping the original in a 'cause' property. effect(globalErrorInEffectFailure)
    })
    
    // Should trigger diagnostic - JSON.parse inside Effect.gen
    export const parseInGen = Effect.gen(function*() {
      const text = yield* Effect.succeed("{\"name\":\"Test\"}")
      const parsed = JSON.parse(text)
                     ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! suggestion TS377026: Consider using Effect Schema for JSON operations instead of JSON.parse/JSON.stringify. effect(preferSchemaOverJson)
      //             ^- should suggest using Effect Schema
      return parsed
    })
    
    // Should trigger diagnostic - JSON.stringify inside Effect.gen
    export const stringifyInGen = Effect.gen(function*() {
      const data = yield* Effect.succeed({ name: "Test" })
      const json = JSON.stringify(data)
                   ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! suggestion TS377026: Consider using Effect Schema for JSON operations instead of JSON.parse/JSON.stringify. effect(preferSchemaOverJson)
      //           ^- should suggest using Effect Schema
      return json
    })
    
    // Should trigger diagnostic - JSON.parse inside Effect.fn
    export const parseInFn = Effect.fn("parseInFn")(function*() {
      const text = "{\"name\":\"FnTest\"}"
      const parsed = JSON.parse(text)
                     ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! suggestion TS377026: Consider using Effect Schema for JSON operations instead of JSON.parse/JSON.stringify. effect(preferSchemaOverJson)
      //             ^- should suggest using Effect Schema
      return parsed
    })
    
    // Should NOT trigger - JSON.parse in regular function
    const _regularParse = () => {
      const text = "{\"name\":\"Regular\"}"
      return JSON.parse(text)
    }
    
    // Should NOT trigger - JSON.parse inside nested function in Effect.gen
    export const nestedFunctionInGen = Effect.gen(function*() {
      const parseFn = () => JSON.parse("{\"nested\":true}")
      //                    ^- this is inside a nested function, should NOT trigger
      return parseFn()
    })
    
    // Should NOT trigger - JSON.parse not as direct expression in Effect.try
    export const notDirectExpression = Effect.try(() => {
      const parsed = JSON.parse("{\"indirect\":true}")
      return parsed.indirect
    })
